The polynomial equation is $ -2t^3 + 15t^2 + 36t + 100 = 10 $. Subtract 10 from both sides to get $ -2t^3 + 15t^2 + 36t + 90 = 0 $. Factor the equation: $ -2t^3 + 15t^2 + 36t + 90 = 0 $. Factor out a -2: $ -2(t^3 - 7.5t^2 - 18t - 45) = 0 $. So, $ t^3 - 7.5t^2 - 18t - 45 = 0 $. Use rational root theorem to find possible roots. Try $ t = 5 $: $ 125 - 187.5 - 90 - 45 = -197.5 $. Not a root. Try $ t = 6 $: $ 216 - 270 - 108 - 45 = -197 $. Not a root. Try $ t = 7 $: $ 343 - 367.5 - 126 - 45 = -295.5 $. Not a root. Try $ t = 9 $: $ 729 - 583.5 - 162 - 45 = 6.5 $. Close. Try $ t = 8 $: $ 512 - 480 - 144 - 45 = -157 $. Not a root. Try $ t = 10 $: $ 1000 - 750 - 180 - 45 = 95 $. Not a root. So, the equation has no rational roots. Use numerical methods or graphing to approximate the solution. The function $ V(t) $ is a cubic polynomial with leading coefficient negative, so it will tend to $ -\infty $ as $ t \to \infty $ and $ +\infty $ as $ t \to -\infty $. The function crosses the line $ V(t) = 10 $ at one point. The approximate solution is $ t \approx 10 $.
